title: Keep secrets out of HCL and state
description: Never hardcode credentials in .tf files; inject via variables/secret managers and treat the state file itself as secret-bearing.
body: Use when a resource needs a password, key, or token. Do not put secrets in .tf source, in variable defaults, or in committed tfvars, and do not assume state is safe to share. Actions: pass secrets via environment variables or a secret manager data source, mark variables and outputs sensitive, and restrict and encrypt the state backend because Terraform stores resource attributes (including secrets) in plaintext state. Validation signals include grep of source/tfvars finding no literal secrets and an encrypted, access-restricted backend. Risk boundary: a leaked state file exposes every secret it recorded; treat state exposure as a credential-rotation incident.
